revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-709/ Iwata city tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bsinpouin.pdf Iwata City Department of Education pamphlet] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Tumulus No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji ware]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-709/ Iwata city tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bsinpouin.pdf Iwata City Department of Education pamphlet] {{in lang|ja}}

Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-709/ Iwata city tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bsinpouin.pdf Iwata City Department of Education pamphlet] {{in lang|ja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504126]] Wikidata name: Shinpōinyama Kofun group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.771167|N|137.873111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Iwata|qid=Q386651}}| region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504126]] Wikidata name: Shinpōinyama Kofun group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.771167|N|137.873111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Iwata|qid=Q386651}}| region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shinpōinyama Kofun group]][[ja:新豊院山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504126]] Wikidata name: Shinpōinyama Kofun group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.771167|N|137.873111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}}| region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shinpōinyama Kofun group]][[ja:新豊院山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504126]] Wikidata name: Shinpōinyama Kofun group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinpōinyama Kofun group | native_name = 新豊院山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinpōinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|46|16|N|137|52|26|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.771167|N|137.873111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}}| region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun period | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Limited (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shinpōinyama Kofun group'''|新豊院山古墳群|Shinpōinyama kofun-gun}} is cluster of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s dating from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi]] to the early [[Kofun period]] located in the Mukasatakenouchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1987.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138484|title=新豊院山古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinpōinyama Kofun group is located on a low hill at the confluence of the [[Tenryū River]] with the Ota River on the eastern edge of the Iwatabara plateau. The necropolis consists of over 30 tumuli in various styles, which were arbitrarily divided into Group A through Group D by archaeologists. Due to construction work, Group D was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980. It was found to contain two keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) and one square tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) from the Kofun period and three graves from the earlier Yayoi period, along with a large amount of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] shards. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the keyhole-shaped tumuli, designated No.2, has a total length of 28 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 17 meters and anterior rectangular portion width of 11 meters. It was constructed in the early 4th century based on excavated pottery, making it one of the oldest in the prefecture. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber with alternate layers of clay and gravel, measuring 8.1 meters by 5.6 meters, containing a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] with a wooden coffin. Inside were numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a bronze hammer, iron swords, and Chinese-made [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a triangular rim and design of mythical animals, along with pottery from the early Kofun period. These items indicate a strong connection with the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun No.3 located to the west is the square tumulus, measuring 12 meters on each side. It was found to contain an iron sword and spear, around a wooden coffin, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery from the end of the third century. The No.1 tumulus is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 34 meters. It has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from "Kasaume" bus stop on the Entetsu bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]], and us located on a hill behind a Buddhist temple, with limited public access.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-709/ Iwata city tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bsinpouin.pdf Iwata City Department of Education pamphlet] {{in lang|ja}}
